mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.15% represents near-zero policy rates in a financial crisis. As credit markets remain frozen and deflation risk dominates, the 10-year Treasury yield will fall further as investors flee to safety and the Fed maintains emergency accommodation. Declining FEDFUNDS signals persistent downward pressure on long-term yields.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicates zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: financial crisis with zero lower bound monetary policy
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day forward window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 7.6% and declining payroll employment (PAYEMS at 134,580k) signal accelerating labor market deterioration. Recession deepens confidence collapse, forcing investors into long-duration safe assets. Rising unemployment directly predicts lower 10-year yields as growth expectations collapse and flight-to-safety intensifies.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% in Feb 2009 during Great Recession
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 10 basis points in months when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp and PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 344,649M) and housing starts (HOUST at 550k) are collapsing as consumer demand evaporates. Aggregate demand destruction forces the Fed to maintain ultra-low rates and signals deflationary pressure. Lower expected future growth rates compress long-term Treasury yields as investors reprice terminal growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month OR HOUST falls below 600k units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550k in Dec 2008, near historic lows
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or positive correlation between demand weakness and yield changes breaks down (Spearman rho > -0.2) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 103.6) is declining sharply as manufacturing collapses during the recession. Supply-side weakness combined with demand destruction creates deflationary environment. Lower inflation expectations and reduced economic slack push 10-year Treasury yields downward as real rates compress and nominal growth forecasts fall.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines month-over-month but 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 5 basis points over the next 5 trading days in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL at 211.49) is falling as demand collapses and commodity prices crash. Deflation risk triggers safe-haven demand for long-duration Treasuries. The combination of zero rates (FEDFUNDS 0.15%) and falling inflation expectations creates a regime where nominal yields must fall to maintain positive real rates, pushing 10-year yields lower.
regime_hint: deflation risk with zero lower bound policy
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL declines and FEDFUNDS is near zero, 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days in more than 35% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 11,599.4B) contracted sharply in Q4 2008 and continues declining into Q1 2009. Negative growth momentum forces investors to extend duration and accept lower yields. The recession deepens expectations of prolonged economic weakness, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as terminal growth rates are revised lower.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 contracted in Q4 2008 and Q1 2009
failure_condition: GDPC1 remains negative or flat but 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 30 basis points over 60 trading days in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 7.6% combined with collapsing payroll employment signals severe labor slack. This slack eliminates inflation risk and forces the Fed to maintain accommodative policy indefinitely. With no inflation threat and massive unemployment, 10-year Treasury yields compress downward as investors price in years of below-trend growth and subdued wage pressures.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 15 basis points when UNRATE > 7.0% over the next 20 trading days in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 550k) are at depression levels while retail sales (RSAFS) collapse simultaneously. This dual demand shock signals that both residential and consumer spending have fallen off a cliff. The combined shock forces the Fed to cut rates further and investors to flee to long-duration Treasuries, pushing 10-year yields downward as recession deepens.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and retail demand collapsed in early 2009
regime_hint: synchronized demand collapse across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both HOUST < 600k and RSAFS declines > 1.5% simultaneously, 10-year Treasury yield increases by more than 20 basis points over the next 20 trading days in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.
